text:
They never got the doughnut holes out of the bag.
Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain spoke to President Trump on Sunday evening about the importance of reopening the Strait of Hormuz, according to a spokeswoman for the prime minister. The shipping route has been all but closed by Iran, seriously disrupting global trade, particularly in oil.
The spokeswoman said Starmer had also offered Trump his condolences over the deaths of at least 13 U.S. military personnel who have died in the war.
The Israeli military has no goal of “overthrowing” the government in Iran, Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin, the spokesman for the Israeli military, said in a news briefing on Sunday.
His comment echoes that of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, who said on Thursday that the collapse of Iran’s autocratic government ultimately depends on the Iranian people.
title: Trump adds to speculation about Iranian leader’s condition.

citability.title: Trump adds to speculation about Iranian leader’s condition.
